definition: I2S assembles a well-formed protocol or grammar string scaffold from individually-intercepted string comparisons (HTTP status-line and header tokens for curl, XML prolog and namespace keywords for libxml2, SQL statement keywords for sqlite3), advancing a state machine deep enough for downstream gates to fire.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Bytes forming a structurally valid URL: a scheme/`://` separator, a numeric port, and an HTTP version token, so curl actually creates and caches a connection that is later torn down via Curl_conncache_close_all_connections, which calls Curl_conncache_remove_conn with lock=TRUE — taking the true side of `if(lock)`.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry a literal `://` separator at offsets 0x0a-0x0c, a `:9001` port followed by `/850`, and a `HTTP/27.0` version token at 0x24-0x2c, i.e. a parseable connection target whose teardown reaches the locked-remove path (the W-only CONNCACHE_LOCK lines 284/295 are hit).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds are short (35-73 bytes) with `://` replaced by run-length `%`/`.`/`+` filler and no port or HTTP-version token, so no real connection is cached and the false (lock=0) side dominates (L hits L283 F=20, never the true side).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the exact comparison operands (scheme/`://`, port `9001`, `HTTP/` token) that the URL parser compares against, building the structured connection target by construction; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must stumble onto these multi-byte literals via blind havoc, which they fail to do within budget.

hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive losers are short SELECT 1; / garbage inputs (9-49 bytes) with no operator-joined literal run; they never take the true branch (L L102982 T=0 F=32).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes one CMP operand for the other at matching offsets, planting the integer-token byte patterns the parser/codegen compares against; the winner seed's ancestor chain contains I2S-floor dash rows.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where cmplog still fails to take codeInteger's true branch when the integer literal it codegens is replaced by a single fixed constant with no comparison against input bytes (no CMP for I2S to mine), while value_profile/minimizer also fail — showing the divergence is unrelated to integer-literal CMP introspection or energy.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The value_profile and minimizer mechanism attributions rest on hitcount/trial-vector divergence and generic technique knowledge rather than a direct byte-level CMP_MAP or per-seed energy trace at this specific branch.

signature.mechanism_summary: cmplog intercepts character-class CMP callbacks inside sqlite3GetToken and I2SRandReplace substitutes the expected token bytes ('SELECT ' prefix and arithmetic chars) into the input, building the valid token stream that naive's blind havoc cannot assemble.
signature.one_line: I2S assembles 'SELECT ' prefix and arithmetic token stream for tokenizer

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must begin with the literal SQL keyword 'SELECT ' and a well-formed arithmetic token stream so sqlite3GetToken takes the true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Bytes at offsets 0x00-0x06 must spell the SQL token 'SELECT ' (53 45 4c 45 43 54 20) followed by a lexically valid arithmetic-token stream (digits, '+','-','.', '\r') so sqlite3GetToken classifies the next character into the token class whose true branch at line 159609 is exercised.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All 5 cmplog winner seeds start with the exact bytes '53 45 4c 45 43 54 20' (SELECT followed by space) and continue with '8+3' arithmetic plus repeating digit/'+'/'-'/'.' bytes; the byte-diff shows offsets 0x00-0x06 are uniformly S,E,L,E,C,T,space across all 9 winner instances.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds are short malformed strings (e.g. '111E.T', '1ES...', '9999EEEE...') that never reconstruct the 'SELECT ' prefix or a valid arithmetic token sequence, so the tokenizer never reaches the true side at 159609.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog intercepts the integer/string CMP callbacks inside sqlite3GetToken's character-class comparisons and records both operands; I2SRandReplace then substitutes the expected token bytes (SELECT keyword chars and operator chars) into the input, building the valid token stream that naive's blind havoc cannot assemble.
